title: C For Loop
content:
### For Loop in C

The `for` loop is ideal when the number of iterations is known in advance.

### Syntax

```c
for (initialization; condition; update) {
  // code to repeat
}
```

### Execution Flow

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Initialization executes (once) |
| 2 | Condition is evaluated |
| 3 | If true, loop body executes |
| 4 | Update executes |
| 5 | Go back to step 2 |

### Variations

| Variation | Example |
|-----------|---------|
| Multiple variables | `for (int i = 0, j = 10; i < j; i++, j--)` |
| Empty initialization | `for (; i < 10; i++)` |
| Empty condition (infinite) | `for (;;)` |
| Decrementing | `for (i = 10; i > 0; i--)` |
| Stepping by 2 | `for (i = 0; i < 10; i += 2)` |

### Scope in C99/C11

In C99 and later, you can declare the loop variable inside the `for` statement. Its scope is limited to the loop.

---

> **Key Point:** The `for` loop is best for counted iterations. All three parts (initialization, condition, update) are optional but the semicolons are required.

quiz.question: How many times does the initialization part of a for loop execute?
